exemplar.title:
Using kinematics, how do variations in a velocity-time graph between Charles
Leclerc and Oscar Piastri during Turn 1 of the 2024 Azerbaijan Grand Prix
explain differences in their cornering performance?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear investigative sequence with logical flow through rationale, modelling, results and conclusion
Effective use of headings, subâheadings, numbered sections and a table of contents to organize content
Material is predominantly relevant to the research question
weaknesses: Occasional narrative digressions interrupt the logical development
Repetition of definitions (derivative, integral) reduces conciseness
Raw data tables split across pages and inconsistent citation formatting detract from organization
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and follows a clear structure from introduction through to conclusion, with appropriate transitional text and labelled figures (band 3). Minor organizational issues (split tables, duplicated definitions) and occasional narrative digressions prevent it from reaching the highest band. A score of 3 reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Key formulae are presented with correct symbols and variables defined
Use of multiple representations (scatterplots, logistic/polynomial curves, integration diagrams) is effective
Explanations generally reference the accompanying mathematical representations
weaknesses: Inconsistent formatting of negative signs and exponents in pasted software output
Axis labels or units are sometimes missing or faint on graphs
Excessive numerical precision and inconsistent significant figures hinder clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication is mostly clear and uses appropriate language and notation in places, but recurring inconsistencies in formatting, missing units on axes, and variable numerical precision limit overall clarity and consistency (between bands 2 and 3). A score of 2 is appropriate given these persistent lapses.

strengths: Strong personal motivation established through the studentâs passion for F1
Clear justification of dataâcollection and modelling choices shows independent thinking
Creative initiative in blending logistic and high-degree polynomial models
weaknesses: Mathematical methods largely follow standard procedures without further extension
No advanced or novel parameterâestimation techniques were developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, with evidence of creativity (band 2). However, it does not reach outstanding originality or initiative, so a score of 2 reflects substantial but not exceptional personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on model limitations, data anomalies and their impact
Clear links made between mathematical results and racing understanding
Justification of outlier removal demonstrates thoughtful analysis of data quality
weaknesses: Reflection lacks deeper statistical diagnostics or sensitivity analyses
Critical analysis is present but not penetrating or rigorous
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and some critique, connecting findings back to the research question (band 2). While thoughtful, it does not achieve the depth or critical rigour required for the top band, so a score of 2 is awarded.

strengths: Application of HL-level mathematics (logistic modelling, 8th-degree polynomials, differentiation, integration) appropriate to the exploration
Analytic differentiation and definite integrals are set up correctly
Conceptual links between calculus techniques and kinematic interpretation are clear
weaknesses: Minor recurring accuracy errors (logistic derivative sign, missing units)
Mathematical methods are routine with limited justification of model choice (no fit statistics)
Some arithmetic slips in manual coefficient computation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant at HL course level, mostly correct, and shows good understanding in parts, but minor errors and a lack of rigorous quantitative justification keep it in band 3. A score of 3 reflects this solid but not fully sophisticated use of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personally meaningful F1 scenario, deciding to capture telemetry manually and devising a bespoke piecewise model all demonstrate significant independent thinking.  However, much of the mathematical technique follows standard modelling steps without further original extension, so evidence is strong but not âoutstandingâ.

The student shows good conceptual grasp of how calculus links to kinematics and articulates why two different function types are needed.  Nonetheless, the rationale for selecting an 8th-degree polynomial or for manual parameter values is thin, indicating good but not thorough understanding.

Derivative and integral expressions appear correct in form, and numerical answers are plausible.  Yet the logistic derivative sign error (extra negative) and absence of units in several force values point to small but recurring inaccuracies, matching the âmostly correctâ band.

Most material is relevant to the aim, but there is noticeable repetition (multiple re-definitions of derivative/integral; re-stating what polynomial degree means) and some long narrative digressions on F1 regulations.  The exploration therefore feels slightly longer than necessary to achieve its purpose, matching the âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ description.

Key formulae are written with appropriate symbols, derivatives and integrals are expressed correctly and variables are defined.  However, algebraic working is sometimes pasted directly from software with limited symbolic tidying, and negative signs/exponents are occasionally mis-formatted, so consistency falls short of the highest standard.

Explanations normally reference the accompanying representation, and notation is fairly stable throughout, giving mostly clear communication.  Minor lapses (switching between l(x) and l1(x); typographical slips in exponent formatting) introduce small inconsistencies that place the work in the âclear and mostly consistentâ band.

Logistic growth/decay, 8th-degree polynomial modelling, analytic differentiation and definite integration are all at HL course level and used appropriately to address the question.  However, the mathematics is largely routine once the model form is chosen, so it is ârelevantâ¦ usedâ rather than âused effectivelyâ.

The student supplies tables of raw data, scatterplots, logistic and polynomial curves, acceleration graphs and shaded integration diagramsâclearly more than one representation.  Graphs are generally labelled, though axes units occasionally disappear and some screenshots are blurry; thus the use is effective rather than sophisticated.

The student reflects on results by linking area under the curve to distance gained, and comments on how braking styles influence lap time.  Limitations of models and fuel assumptions are acknowledged.  Reflection is meaningful but not penetratingly critical, hence âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ.

Blending logistic and high-degree polynomial models, plus a self-created force table, shows creativity and initiative beyond minimal requirements.  Yet the mathematical novelty is moderate (no optimisation or parameter estimation techniques), so the work fits solidly in the second band.

The report follows a clear investigative sequenceâfrom rationale, through data collection, modelling, manipulation, and ending with conclusion and limitations.  Headings and sub-headings guide the reader logically, and transitional sentences usually link sections.  Nevertheless, occasional digressions (e.g. lengthy F1 background, duplicated explanations of sampling and fuel assumptions) interrupt the flow, so the development is clear rather than exemplary.

Analysis compares parameters (L, k), acceleration extremes and integrated distances, offering some critique of each driverâs approach.  While thoughtful, it stops short of rigorous statistical or sensitivity analysis, so the depth aligns with the second band.

Explanations connect mathematical findings to racing understanding (e.g. smoother braking translates to higher exit speed).  This clearly links back to the stated aim and shows the studentâs learning, but the enhancement of mathematical understanding is moderate rather than strong.

A front-page, table of contents, numbered sections and labelled figures give the work a recognisable introductionâbodyâconclusion structure.  Sections appear in an expected order and graphs/tables are embedded near the accompanying text.  Minor organisational weaknesses (for example, two separate calculation sections that could be merged, and tables split across pages) prevent the seamless feel required for the top band.

The studentâs voice is evident: they justify data-collection choices, comment on anomalies, and articulate why smoothness matters in racing.  This drives the exploration rather than simply accompanying it, meeting the descriptor for a âclear personal approachâ.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated, specifying the kinematics context and drivers compared. Consider defining variables like velocity in m/s in this section to enhance clarity.
The table of contents effectively maps sections to page numbers, improving navigation. To enhance readability, align section titles and their page numbers consistently across columns.
The introduction shows strong personal engagement: the student integrates their passion for F1 with classroom kinematics, driving a clear motivation for the exploration.
Background on F1 regulations is extensive but partially distracts from the mathematical focus. Trim narrative digressions to improve conciseness and maintain relevance.
The modeling paragraph clearly explains why logistic and polynomial functions are chosen and how piecewise modeling applies here, demonstrating sound mathematical communication.
Repeated definitions of derivatives and integrals later in the report reduce conciseness. Merge these explanations into a single, concise overview.
The sampling method is well described: systematic sampling at every 5 frames is appropriate, and the conversion from km/h to m/s is clearly shown.
The raw data tables are split across the page and interrupt the flow. Combine or position tables closer to the narrative to improve organization.
Decimal precision varies across the tables. Standardize the number of significant figures to improve mathematical consistency and readability.
Identification and removal of anomalous points is justified with clear visual reasoning, demonstrating meaningful reflection on data quality.
Anomaly removal remains qualitative. Incorporate a statistical criterion (e.g., interquartile range) for outlier detection to increase rigor.
The anomaly graph is clear, but some axis labels and units are faint or missing. Ensure all graph axes include legible labels and units.
The general logistic formula is presented correctly. Explicitly note the sign convention in the exponent (âkx) to avoid confusion in interpretation.
Parameter fitting is done via GDC but lacks description of the fitting criterion. Briefly describe how best fit was determined (e.g., least squares) to improve transparency.
Logistic model parameter values are reported to many decimal places. Round parameters to three significant figures for clearer presentation.
Linking parameter k to deceleration rate is insightful; including its unit (sâ»Â¹) explicitly would improve the precision of interpretation.
The degree-8 polynomial coefficients are displayed with excessive precision, which hinders readability. Consider rounding to three significant figures.
The rationale for choosing an 8th-degree polynomial lacks quantitative support. Include model fit statistics (e.g., RÂ² or SSE) to justify degree selection.
Graph 6 effectively overlays logistic and polynomial models but the axes lack clear units and labels. Label both axes and include units (s, m/s).
The piecewise modeling rationale is articulated clearly. For completeness, state the exact domain intervals for each piece in the narrative.
The piecewise function is well formatted but uses inconsistent interval notation (â¤ versus <). Standardize this to ensure mathematical precision.
Quantitative evaluation of the continuity of piecewise functions (e.g., matching derivatives at the join point) would deepen the analysis of smooth transitions.
Differentiation rules are presented concisely in Formula 2. This general approach is communicated clearly and consistently.
Check the arithmetic of one differentiated coefficient (e.g., the xâ´ term) to ensure no minor slip occurred in the manual computation.
Quotientârule derivation of the logistic derivative is shown step-by-step, enhancing clarity of methodology.
The substituted logistic derivative shows a negativeâsign inconsistency compared to standard form; verify the exponential term handling to correct this sign error.
Overlaid acceleration curves in Graph 12 facilitate comparison of braking and acceleration profiles. Adding a legend directly on the plot would further improve readability.
Linking peak acceleration values to driving technique is insightful; present those numeric extremes in a small table for quick reference.
Definite integrals are correctly set up for each phase. Explicitly state the resulting units (meters) when reporting the integrated distances.
Comparison of integrated distances is clear and quantifies performance difference. Discuss how changing car mass (fuel burn-off) might alter distance outcomes.
Fuel mass assumption is transparent but likely overestimates qualifying load. Conduct a sensitivity check on fuel mass to assess its impact on force calculations.
The forceâtime table is extensive; visualizing force vs. time in a plot would help identify braking and acceleration phases more effectively.
The force equation is stated clearly, but include units for each quantity (m, s, kg) within the formula to maintain consistency.
Summary table of key force metrics effectively highlights comparative performance. Adding percentage differences would quantify the magnitude of those differences.
Conclusion integrates findings well but could explicitly restate the research question to reinforce how results answer it.
Limitations are acknowledged thoughtfully. To strengthen reflection, suggest statistical diagnostics (e.g., residual plots) to evaluate model fit inaccuracies.
Bibliography entries show essential sources but citation formatting is inconsistent. Apply a uniform referencing style and include complete access dates.
